Corporate Profile 6 | About Us 8 | UBA's History and Key Milestones 9 | UBA's Global Footprint 14 | Board of Directors

About Us UBA is "Africa's Global Bank" providing Commercial Banking, Pension Custody and Related Financial Services to its over 45 million corporate, commercial and retail customers, served through robust physical and electronic touch-points. Our Vision To be the undisputed leading and dominant financial services institution in Africa. Our Mission To be a role model for African businesses by creating superior value for all stakeholders, abiding by the utmost professional and ethical standards, and building an enduring institution. United Bank For Africa Plc // 2025 Annual Report and Accounts

Corporate Profile United Bank for Africa Plc (UBA) is a leading pan-African financial services group with presence in 20 African countries, as well as the United Kingdom, the UnitedStates of America, France and United Arab Emirates. About UBA UBA was incorporated in Nigeria as a limited liability company after taking over the assets of the British and French Bank Limited, which had been operating in Nigeria since 1949. The United Bank for Africa merged with Standard Trust Bank in 2005. From a single country operation founded in 1949 in Nigeria - Africa's largest economy, UBA has become one of the top providers of banking and other financial services on the African continent. The Bank provides services to over 45 million customers globally, through one of the most diverse service channels in Sub-Saharan Africa with over 1,000 branches and customer touch points and a robust online and mobile banking platform. The shares of UBA are publicly traded on the premium board of the Nigerian Exchange Limited (NGX) and the Bank has a well-diversified shareholder base, which includes foreign and local institutional investors, as well as individual shareholders. PRODUCTS UBA is a financial institution, offering a range of banking and pension fund custody services. MARKET UBA has over 45 million customers in retail, commercial and corporate market segments spanning across 24 countries, consisting of Nigeria, 19 other African countries, the United States of America, the United Kingdom, France and United Arab Emirates. ENTERPRISE • Own the task • Go the extra mile, solve problems • Show initiative • Break barriers • Be innovative EXCELLENCE • Be responsive and passionate • Surpass customerʼs expectations always • Maintain quality standards • Be meticulous and make it simple always • Be professional - integrity, friendly and genuine EXECUTION • Get it done • Get it done now • Get it done very well • Always have the end in mind UBA has one of the largest distribution networks in Africa. As at December 31, 2025, there were over 1,000 branches and customer touch points across Africa, 2,209 ATMs and 430,000 POS machines fully deployed. CHANNELS As at December 31, 2025, the Group had over 25,000 direct and support staff. STAFF United Bank For Africa Plc // 2025 Annual Report and Accounts

UBA's History & Key Milestones 2001 - 2010 • Standard Trust Bank commenced operations in Ghana (2004) • UBA merged with Standard Trust Bank and acquired Continental Trust Bank (2005) • Successfully raised fresh equity capital (2007) Raised NGN20 billion debt capital and acquired majority interest in banks in Burkina Faso and the Republic of Benin (2008 - 2011); and also opened the Paris office in 2009 • Commenced operations in other African countries like Kenya, Uganda, Cameroon, Cote dʼIvoire, Sierra Leone and Mozambique 2021 - Date • Celebrating 75 years of banking operation in Africa • Opened UBA Dubai-DIFC to facilitate international corporate relationships • Expanded digital services, including blockchain services for ATM transactions • Launched its virtual account payment service to offer virtual accounts to customers • Achieved biometric authentication and payments on its ATM terminals 1981 - 2000 • UBA established an operation in New York, becoming the first sub-Saharan African Bank to operate in America (1984) • UBAʼs London business became a subsidiary (1984) • Standard Trust bank commenced operations (1997) • UBA becomes the first Nigerian bank to issue Global Depository Receipts (1998) 1949 - 1980 • British & French Bank (BFB) commenced business in Nigeria with its first office in Lagos (1949) • UBA was incorporated as a Limited Liability Company, taking over the assets and liabilities of British & French Bank (1961) • UBA became the first Nigerian Bank to make an Initial Public Offering (1970) 2011 - 2020 • Won Financial Timesʼ Bankersʼ Award for the overall best Bank in Africa, Best Bank in Cameroon and Senegal (2012) • Divested from its non-bank subsidiaries and property management business (2013) • UBA Chad and Senegal awarded Bank of the Year by the Banker Magazine (2014) • Raised $500m 5year senior unsecured Eurobond. • Achieved Premium Board listing on the NSE (2017) • Introduced Leo, an AI-powered chatbot (2018). • Established subsidiary in Mali and named Africaʼs Best Digital Bank by Euromoney (2018) United Bank For Africa Plc // 2025 Annual Report and Accounts

Corporate Profile Benin Cameroon Guinea RDC France www.ubagroup.com/nigeria Nigeria www.ubatanzania.co.tz Tanzania United States of America Zambia Gabon Mali Uganda Ghana Mozambique United Kingdom UAE Congo Brazzaville Kenya Senegal Burkina Faso Chad Côte d'Ivoire Liberia Sierra Leone UBA's Global Footprint United Bank For Africa Plc // 2025 Annual Report and Accounts

UBA's International Presence ... and being a gateway between Africa and the rest of the World United States of America France United Kingdom (UK) United Arab Emirates (UAE) Facilitating payments by leveraging on our unique position, as the only Sub-Saharan African bank with an operational banking license in the US providing US Dollar clearing capabilities. Supporting trade with Africa and Americas, on one hand and Africa and Asia, on the other hand. Supporting capital flows by providing correspondent banking services to African Financial Institutions. Facilitating trade and payments between the US and Africa, by offering FX solutions and pricing for various African currencies to US corporates, financial institutions and supranational entities Provide representation in France and the wider EU to facilitate the onboarding and management of strategic, Corporate and Multinational relationships headquartered in France and across the EU. Bring Africa closer to France and Europe: Be the one point of contact for all financial matters these organizations may have in Africa and provide insight on the macroeconomic and regulatory environment of those countries we operate in. Provide lending and other financing activities and encourage the influx of investors from Europe to Africa and from Africa to Europe. Operating as a branch of Parent Company, UBA Dubai-DIFC was established in July 2022 and has been pivotal to the acceleration of the onboarding of Corporate relationships headquartered in the UAE and wider Gulf region. Promoting Correspondent Banking relationships in the Middle East and to also broaden our Corporate clientele base beyond Dubai & UAE. Serve customers across the Middle-East with major focus on correspondent banking, trade and treasury, whilst harnessing the opportunities in the region to unlock untapped economic potential and support the next phase of development across the continent. UBA UK is a direct conduit for the Nigerian trade sector, supporting the growth in international trade by providing country tailored solutions from an international hub with seamless trade finance services. This is evidenced by the continuous growth in trade transactions during the year 2024. The facilitation of multi-currency cross-border aid and other flows into Nigeria, including our ability to leverage our Group network to deliver "last mile" payments. UBA UK acts as a bridge, optimizing trade across Africa, the UK, Europe and the Middle East. United Bank For Africa Plc // 2025 Annual Report and Accounts

Corporate Profile WHO WE ARE We are focused on supporting people and businesses to succeed across Africa, Europe, Asia and North America. Through our diverse range of products, services and channels, we help people fulfil their goals and enable businesses to prosper. Total Customer Digital Banking Customers Mobile Downloads SME's Accros the Group No. of cards issued Agents ATMs Operations Outside Africa African Operations 2023.4 Active POS Machines Branches and Touchpoints Employees 45 million+ 44.8million + 40.53million + 1.4million + 33 million + 160,000+ 2,209 430,000+ 1,000+ 25,000+ OUR TRACK RECORDS Leo Users 6.8million+ United Bank For Africa Plc // 2025 Annual Report and Accounts

THE BOARD

Standing (From L-R): Ugochukwu Nwaoghodoh, Tosin Adewuyi, Aisha Hassan Baba, Caroline Anyanwu, Emmanuel Nnorom, Henrietta Ugbo, Alhaji Abdulqadir J. Bello, Emmanuel Odartey Lamptey, Chidi Okpala Sitting (From L-R): Angela Aneke, Oliver Alawuba, Tony O. Elumelu, Chukwuma Nweke, Angela Adebayo United Bank For Africa Plc // 2025 Annual Report and Accounts

Corporate Profile OUR BOARD AND EXECUTIVE MANAGEMENT Tony O. Elumelu, CFR CHAIRMAN Tony O. Elumelu is an African investor and philanthropist, and one of the world's most prominent voices on Africa's transformation agenda. He is the founder and Chairman of Heirs Holdings, his family-owned investment company, which invests in strategic sectors of the African economy, including financial services, hospitality, power, energy, technology, and healthcare, as part of its commitment to improving lives and transforming Africa. Tony is also the Chairman of the United Bank for Africa (UBA) Group, which operates in 20 countries across Africa, as well as the United Kingdom, France, the UAE, and the United States. He chairs Nigeria's largest quoted conglomerate, Transcorp Group, whose subsidiaries include Transcorp Power, a leading producer of electricity in West Africa, and Transcorp Hotels Plc, Nigeria's foremost hospitality brand. Together with Heirs Energies, one of the continent's largest natural resources producers, Transcorp Power is executing Africa's foremost integrated energy business, dedicated to ensuring African resources drive and power value creation on the African continent. In 2010, he created The Tony Elumelu Foundation (TEF), the leading philanthropy empowering a new generation of African entrepreneurs across all 54 African countries. Since inception, the Foundation's flagship programme has identified and funded over 20,000 young African entrepreneurs and created a digital ecosystem of over 1.5 million Africans, as part of a ten-year US$100m commitment to fund, mentor, and train young Africans. In 2020, TIME magazine named Tony as one of the 100 Most Influential People in the World and in 2022 TIME again recognised him with its inaugural TIME100 Impact list, honouring him alongside five other global leaders who have gone above and beyond to move their industries - and the world - forward. The Federal Government of Nigeria honoured Tony with the title, Commander of the Order of the Federal Republic, in 2022 for his contribution to Nigeria's economic and social development. He has also been recognised with Belgium's oldest and highest royal order. Tony sits on a number of global boards, including the Global Leadership Council of United Nations Generation Unlimited. Oliver Alawuba GROUP MANAGING DIRECTOR Oliver Alawuba is a seasoned banking professional with over 25 yearsʼ experience in Corporate and Institutional Banking, Consumer Banking, Public Sector, Retail and Commercial Banking, Project Management, Corporate Governance, and overall bank management, where he has demonstrated strong passion for excellence and result-oriented leadership capability. Prior to his current appointment as the Group Managing Director/CEO of UBA Plc, Oliver was at various times, CEO Designate for UBA Côte dʼIvoire, CEO UBA Ghana, Regional CEO, Anglophone Countries, Executive Director, East Bank in Nigeria, Managing Director/CEO UBA Africa, and later Group Deputy Managing Director/ CEO, UBA Africa covering Nigeria and other 19 Subsidiaries in UBA Africa (exNigeria). Presently as GMD/CEO of UBA Plc, he oversees the Global Operations of UBA across Nigeria, 19 UBA Africa Countries, UBA America, UBA United Kingdom, UBA France, and UBA UAE. He is a Fellow of the Nigeria Institute of Management (FNIM), and an Honorary Fellow of the Chartered Institute of Bankers of Nigeria (FCIB) He graduated from Imo State University, Okigwe (Now Abia State University, Uturu), with a BSc in Food Science & Technology in 1988. He also obtained an MSc in Food Technology from the University of Lagos in 1991 and MBA (Banking & Finance) from United Bank For Africa Plc // 2025 Annual Report and Accounts

Our Board and Executive Management | continued... Abdulqadir J. Bello, (FCA) NON-EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR Abdulqadir J. Bello is a Chartered Accountant, has over 30 yearsʼ corporate experience in the banking sector, during which period he held several senior Management positions in various Banks. He also previously served as the Group Chief Credit Officer of UBA and thereafter as the Executive Director in charge of Risk Management for UBA Group. Abdulqadir Bello is the Chairman of the Board Credit Committee and also serves on the Board Risk Management Committee. He obtained a BSc degree in Accounting from Bayero University, Kano in 1984 and ACA from the Institute of Chartered Accountants in 1989. Angela Adebayo NON-EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR Angela Adebayo obtained a BSc Hon (Social Science) from the University of Ibadan in 1979, an MBA from the University of Lagos in 1982, and MPhil (Cantab) in Land Economy from Cambridge University in 1994. Erelu Adebayo was previously the First Lady of Ekiti State and the Chairman of Afriland Properties Plc. She was also the first female Chairman of the Board of WEMABOD Estates. Erelu Adebayo serves on the Boards of Aliko Dangote Foundation, Meyer Paints Plc and Women at Risk International Foundation. She is also a Council Member on the Nigerian Stock Exchange and has worked extensively on real-estate development across Nigeria. Erelu Adebayo is the Founder of Erelu Adebayo Foundation and Erelu Adebayo Children's Home. Angela Aneke INDEPENDENT NON-EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR Angela Aneke is a board advisor, banker and a strategic thinker with over 30 yearsʼ experience in financial services, in the areas of financial control, strategy, transaction banking, corporate banking, retail banking, and governance. Ms. Aneke has held executive management and board positions in several international and regional institutions, including Ecobank Transnational Incorporated, Citigroup and the United Bank for Africa. Ms. Angela holds a Bachelor of Science degree from The American University, Washington DC, USA and became an International Associate of the American Institute of Certified Public Accountants. She also obtained a Master of Business Administration from Warwick Business School, University of Warwick, UK. She has attended several courses in banking and governance including Harvard Business Schoolʼs "Making Corporate Boards More Effective" and Whartonʼs University of Pennsylvaniaʼs Corporate Governance: "Maximize your effectiveness in the Board room." She is currently the Chairman of the Board Audit, Governance, Nomination & Remuneration Committee and also serves on the Board Credit Committee. United Bank For Africa Plc // 2025 Annual Report and Accounts

Corporate Profile Aisha Hassan Baba, OON INDEPENDENT NON-EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR Aisha Hassan Baba is the Founding and Managing Partner of EBO, HASSAN BABA & CO. Aisha was admitted to practice law in Nigeria in 1981 and in the ensuing 35 years thereafter, served in very senior and sensitive positions in both federal and state public service of the Federal Republic of Nigeria, notably as Deputy Director Public Prosecution, Director, Legal Services under the Federal Ministry of Justice, Federal Ministry of Education, Federal Ministry of Industry, Trade & Investment, Chief Executive Officer (CEO) of the Federal Legal Aid Council (now Commission), Executive Secretary, Nigerian Investment Promotion Commission, and as the Attorney General and Commissioner for Justice, Anambra State. Aisha is a trained Legal Draftsman, contract negotiator and has worked as Co-Chair of the Committee on High Profile Federal Bills, notably the production of the final draft copy of the National Competition and Consumer Protection Bill and the National Competition and Consumer Protection Policy in 2014/15. She has chaired several committees responsible for drafting a number of policies ranging from Nigerian Local Content in the non-oil Sector Policy 2014 to ; a model draft Automotive Bill for the Nigerian Automotive Council (as it then was) to finalise the draft Nigerian Insolvency Bill and so on. In recognition of her diligence, passion and contribution to the public service of the Federation, Aisha was conferred with the National Productivity Order of Merit Award in 2001 and the National Honours, Officer of the Order of the Niger, (OON) in 2005. Aisha is a member of the Nigerian Bar Association, Commonwealth Bar Association, Member, Chartered Institute of Arbitrators UK. Aisha currently serves on the Board Audit, Governance, Nomination & Remuneration Committee. Our Board and Executive Management | continued... Caroline Anyanwu NON-EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR Caroline Anyanwu has over 30 yearsʼ experience in the Banking Industry, covering Strategic Planning,Financial Control, Retail & Commercial Banking, Banking Operations and Risk Management. She is a First Class graduate of Statistics from University of Ilorin in 1984, a Fellow of the Institute of Chartered Accountants (ICAN), and a Prize Winner in the ICAN Professional Qualifying Examination (overall 2nd). She obtained top-rated core basic and intermediate management competencies as a Trainee Accountant in Price Waterhouse (Chartered Accountants) - now PricewaterhouseCoopers (PwC). Caroline who previously occupied the position of Head, Credit Risk Management at United Bank for Africa Plc, has worked at senior level positions with several international financial institutions. An Honorary Senior Member of the Chartered Institute of Bankers of Nigeria, an Associate Member of the Chartered Institute of Taxation of Nigeria, and a member of Caroline Anyanwu the Risk Management Association of Nigeria. United Bank For Africa Plc // 2025 Annual Report and Accounts

Chukwuma Nweke DEPUTY MANAGING DIRECTOR, OPERATIONS, TECHNOLOGY & DIGITAL BANKING Chukwuma Nweke has over 30 years of experience across Banking Operations, Finance, Technology, Audit, Strategy, Retail Banking, and Payments, Mr. Nweke previously served as Executive Director and Group Chief Operating Officer of UBA Plc. Mr. Nweke holds a Bachelorʼs degree in Accountancy and an MBA from the University of Nigeria, Nsukka. He is a Fellow of the Institute of Chartered Accountants of Nigeria (FCA) and an honorary member of the Chartered Institute of Bankers of Nigeria. Henrietta Ugboh INDEPENDENT NON-EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR Henrietta Ugboh is a board advisor, financial literacy and risk management trainer, mentor and banker with 30 yearsʼ experience in financial services, in the areas of corporate and commercial banking, credit and risk management, private banking and governance. Mrs. Ugboh has held senior management and board positions in several institutions, including Citibank Nigeria Limited, Investment One Financial Services, Harvard Business School Association of Nigeria (HBSAN) and Ziphii Technologies. Hetty holds a Bachelor of Science degree in Economics and Statistics from the University of Benin, Benin City and a Masters of Business Administration from Enugu State University of Technology (ESUT) Business School. She is an alumna of the prestigious Harvard Business School, Boston and attended several courses in banking, leadership and governance including Citibank Country Managerʼs Forum (New York), Citigroup Risk and Compliance trainings, John Maxwell leadership Certification Program (Florida), Institute of Directors - Company Direction Course (Lagos) and African Angel Academy training. She is an Honorary Senior Member of the Chartered Institute of Bankers of Nigeria, Member, Institute of Directors and Fellow of the Institute of Credit Administration (FICA). Emmanuel Nnorom NON-EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR Emmanuel Nnorom is a qualified Chartered Accountant and has over 4 decades of professional experience in the corporate and financial sectors, working with publicly listed companies. He is an alumnus of Oxford Universityʼs Templeton College (1996), and a prize winner at the Foundation, Professional Examinations 1 and Profession Examinations 2 qualifying examinations in 1980,1981 and 1982 and Fellow of the Institute of Chartered Accountants of Nigeria. He trained at Peak Marwick Casselton Elliot (later Peat Marwick, Ani, Ogunde & Co) from 1978 to 1982 and also worked with Nigerian Breweries Plc as well as several financial institutions before retiring from Standard Trust Bank/UBA Plc. Emmanuel N. Nnorom is the CEO of Heirs Holdings Ltd. Prior to joining the Heirs Holdings Group, he served as the CEO of Transnational Corporation of Nigeria Plc, overseeing the Transcorp businesses, including Transcorp Power, Transcorp Hilton Hotels Abuja, Transcorp Hotel, Calabar and Transcorp Energy. Prior to Transcorp, Emmanuel had held senior positions at Heirs Holdings and had served as an Executive Director at UBA Plc and Managing Director of UBA Africa, overseeing the Groupʼs African subsidiaries and executing corporate strategy in 18 African countries.Other senior roles within UBA Plc included Group COO UBA Plc, followed by his appointment as UBAʼs Group CFO, with responsibility for Finance and Risk. Our Board and Executive Management | continued... United Bank For Africa Plc // 2025 Annual Report and Accounts

Corporate Profile Ugochukwu Nwaghodoh EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR, FINANCE AND RISK MANAGEMENT Ugochukwu Nwaghodoh has over 30 years of multifunctional experience spanning banking, advisory and assurance services. Prior to his current role, he was at different times, Group Financial Controller, Group Chief Compliance Officer, and Head - Performance Management at the UBA Group. Before joining UBA in 2004, he had a decade of experience with renowned firms of Deloitte and PricewaterhouseCoopers in Nigeria and Kenya. He holds a BSc in Human Nutrition from the University of Ibadan, MSc in Finance and Management from Cranfield University, and an MSc in Risk Management from New York University. He is an alumnus of the Oxford University's Said Business School, and Institute of Management Development (IMD) Switzerland. Ugo is a fellow of the Institute of Chartered Accountants of Nigeria, a fellow of the Chartered Institute of Taxation of Nigeria (CITN), an Associate member of the Chartered Institute of Stockbrokers of Nigeria, a member of Chartered Institute for Securities and Investments, United Kingdom and an Honorary Senior Member of the Chartered Institute of Bankers of Nigeria (CIBN). He is also a member of the Institute of Directors of Nigeria and holds the Financial Times Non-Executive Director Diploma. Bili A. Odum GROUP COMPANY SECRETARY/ LEGAL COUNSEL Bili A. Odum holds an LLB (Hons) degree from Edo State University, Ekpoma, Nigeria and was enrolled as a Solicitor and Advocate of the Supreme Court of Nigeria in 1990. He is a member of the Chartered Institute of Arbitrators (United Kingdom), the Nigerian Bar Association and the International Bar Association. He is an alumnus of the Lagos Business School (Chief Executive Programme 18) and the New York Institute of Finance. He has held high-level strategic positions in top financial service institutions in Nigeria, with responsibilities that encompass Asset Management, Structured Finance, Legal Advisory, Corporate Governance, Human Resources Management, Administration, Knowledge Management and Business Communication. Our Board and Executive Management | continued... United Bank For Africa Plc // 2025 Annual Report and Accounts

Strategy & 22 | Chairman's Statement 26 | Chief Executive Officer's Report

Tony O. Elumelu Chairman, Board of Directors

Strategy & Business Review Chairman's Statement The successful conclusion of our capital raise, further strengthens the Group's capital base and positions the Group to pursue growth opportunities across our markets STRATEGIC OVERVIEW Dear Fellow Shareholders, It is my pleasure to present the 2025 Annual Report and Financial Statements of United Bank for Africa Plc. 2025 saw continued strategic progress by the Group. Despite a complex environment, UBA strengthened its capital position and advanced its long-term growth strategy in Africa and our global markets. Our results reflect the resilience of our diversified business model, disciplined risk management, and the fundamental value of our pan-African platform, connecting businesses, governments, and individuals across the continent. The successful conclusion of our capital raise, further strengthens the Group's capital base and positions the Group to pursue growth opportunities across our markets. The consistent support of shareholders reflects continued confidence in UBA's financial strength, governance standards, and long-term strategy. 2025 also represented an important inflection point for Africa's economic and financial landscape. Africa saw improved macroeconomic stability, stronger growth momentum, and recovering commodity prices. At the same time, central banks across the region began recalibrating monetary policy in response to moderating inflationary pressures. Against this backdrop, UBA remains focused on delivering sustainable earnings growth. We continue to invest in our core franchise and digital infrastructure, while deepening customer relationships and expanding our product offerings. We earn the trust of our customers and stakeholders by maintaining a culture that places service excellence, reliability, and long-term partnership at the centre of everything we do. I saw this in person as I undertook a tour across East, Central, and Southern Africa to strengthen those partnerships, catalyse investment opportunities, and reaffirm UBA's commitment to driving economic development and collaboration across the continent. An example of our commitment is our investment in digital innovation through LEO, Africa's first banking chatbot integrated with the PanAfrican Payment and Settlement System (PAPSS), enabling instant cross-border payments in local currencies. Africa's macroeconomic trajectory remains compellingly positive, underpinned by a confluence of structural and cyclical factors that position the continent as one of the most consequential growth frontiers of the coming decade. Africa's long-term investment case is stronger than it has been in a generation, and United Bank for Africa is fulfilling its historic role as the bridge between Africa and the world. ECONOMIC ENVIRONMENT The global economy remained resilient in 2025, despite macroeconomic uncertainties. Global output expanded by approximately 3.3%, supported by improved trade conditions, rising technology investment, and steady domestic demand across major economies. However, geopolitical tensions in Europe and the Middle East and renewed trade frictions between the United States and China continued to create uncertainty, and, most recently, escalating tensions in the Gulf create volatility. In the short term, this will give rise to significant supply chain dislocation, spikes in energy prices and inflationary pressure, reversing gains in taming inflationary pressure. With the IMF projecting sub-Saharan expansion of 4.0 to 4.5% through 2026, the continent is outpacing much of the developed world on the back of easing inflation, greater currency stability, and the deepening reach of the African Continental Free Trade Area. Currency markets across the region also performed positively during the year, supported by stronger export revenues, a softer U.S. dollar, and increasingly credible monetary policy frameworks. In Nigeria, the rebasing of GDP and inflation statistics provided a more accurate reflection of the evolving structure of the economy. Real GDP growth improved to 3.9% in Q3 2025, supported largely by sustained expansion in the non-oil sector. Inflation moderated significantly during the year, declining to 15.5% by December 2025, compared with 34.8% in the previous year, reflecting improved domestic food supply and effective monetary policy measures. United Bank For Africa Plc // 2025 Annual Report and Accounts

Chairman's Statement continued... STRATEGIC PROGRESS AND PERFORMANCE 2025 was a year of tangible strategic delivery for UBA Group. UBA's pan-African network, spanning 20 countries alongside international operations in the US, UK, France, and UAE, continued to differentiate the Group as the pre-eminent connector of African economies to global capital and trade flows. The Group enters 2026 with strong momentum and a clear mandate for the next phase of growth. The Group delivered gross earnings of N3.08 trillion and profit after tax of N423 billion, underscoring the resilience of our customer franchise and earnings base. Profit before tax moderated relative to the prior year, reflecting the impact of broader market conditions on trading income and impairment charges, as the impact of macroeconomic headwinds and end of the forbearance regime on COVID-19 loans took effect. Nonetheless, prudent balance sheet management enabled the Group to maintain profitability and capital strength. Our balance sheet continued to expand, reinforcing the scale and resilience of our franchise. Total deposits increased by 12% to N27.2 trillion, supported by strong customer acquisition, digital banking growth, and increased transaction volumes across our markets. Our loan portfolio stood at N7.45 trillion, reflecting our disciplined risk management philosophy and a deliberate focus on highquality lending across diversified sectors. Total assets increased to N33.17 trillion, while shareholders' funds rose to N4.25 trillion, positioning the Group to support future growth, while maintaining strong capital buffers. Beyond financial performance, UBA continues to strengthen its role as a pan-African financial platform connecting businesses, governments, and consumers across regional and international markets. Strategic investments in digital payments and crossborder transaction infrastructure are expanding sustainable revenue streams and reinforcing the Group's strategic relevance within Africa's financial and trade ecosystem. CAPITAL STRENGTH AND RESILIENCE In March 2024, the Central Bank of Nigeria introduced revised minimum capital requirements aimed at strengthening the resilience of the Nigerian banking sector and enabling banks to better support economic growth. Under this directive, international commercial banks are required to maintain a minimum capital base of N500 billion in share capital and share premium by March 2026. UBA successfully completed its capital raising programme through two tranches of Rights Issues, with the final tranche concluded in September 2025. The Rights Issue offered 3.16 billion ordinary shares at N50 per share to existing shareholders. This milestone positioned UBA well ahead of the regulatory deadline, while preserving strategic flexibility across our international operations. The proceeds will support continued investment in digital technologies, infrastructure, and business expansion across our markets. INTELLECTUAL LEADERSHIP UBA's influence extends well beyond its balance sheet. In July 2025, I delivered the keynote address at the African Caucus of Governors of the World Bank and IMF, calling on African governments, development partners, and the private sector to redefine development on Africa's own terms - homegrown, inclusive, and sustainable. That agenda was given further institutional weight in October, when UBA launched its landmark white paper, Banking on Africa's Future: Unlocking Capital and Partnerships for Sustainable Growth, on the sidelines of the IMF and World Bank Annual Meetings in Washington D.C. The paper provides a comprehensive and actionable framework for unlocking Africa's economic potential across trade facilitation, infrastructure, digital innovation, climate finance, and inclusive growth - while laying out strategies for mobilising domestic capital alongside global partnerships to access the continent's $3.4 trillion single market under AfCFTA. I am proud that UBA is not merely financing Africa's growth, but defining its terms. RESPONSIBLE BANKING: EMBEDDING ESG UBA remains committed to embedding Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) principles across our operations and strategic decision-making. Our Environmental Impact During the year, the Group continued implementing initiatives aimed at supporting climate resilience, sustainable finance, and responsible business practices. UBA Nigeria recorded Scope 1, 2 and 3 emissions of 39,892.41 tCO2e, calculated in line with UNFCCC methodology and Model. In addition, the Group planted 20,490 seedlings in 2025, bringing the total seedlings planted to 30,250, representing a 350% year-on-year increase. Our Social contribution The Group also donated $500,000 to the African Union Peace Fund, supporting initiatives aimed at strengthening peace and security across the continent. UBA contributed N1.98 billion to Corporate Social Responsibility initiatives, including healthcare partnerships such as the Medicaid Cancer Foundation. The Group continues to strengthen its ESG governance framework and will begin voluntary adoption of IFRS S1 and IFRS S2 sustainability disclosure standards from the 2026 financial year. INVESTING IN OUR PEOPLE At UBA, we believe our people are the foundation of our business, they deliver the customer service excellence that we exemplify, they are our ambassadors. Through dedication and teamwork, we are able to deliver excellent service, and shareholder value. Through the Graduate Management Accelerated Programme (GMAP), the Group graduated 1,842 young professionals, United Bank For Africa Plc // 2025 Annual Report and Accounts

Strategy & Business Review Chairman's Statement continued... strengthening our leadership pipeline across Nigeria and Africa. We delivered over 787,000 learning hours to more than 83,000 participants, building capabilities across credit, risk, operations, technology, and artificial intelligence. Additionally, the Group invests in leadership development programmes delivered in partnership with global institutions and the Lagos Business School (LBS) continue to equip our management teams with the strategic capabilities required in an increasingly complex operating environment. EXPANDING OUR AFRICA FOOTPRINT UBA continues to leverage its unique pan-African footprint to support trade, investment, and financial connectivity across the continent. At the IMF and World Bank Annual Meetings 2025 in Washington, D.C., the Group launched its white paper titled: "Banking on Africa's Future: Unlocking Capital and Partnerships for Sustainable Growth." The report highlights the opportunity to mobilise an estimated $4 trillion in Africa's domestic financial assets, including $2.5 trillion in commercial bank assets and more than $1.1 trillion in long-term institutional capital, while leveraging the $3.4 trillion opportunity presented by the African Continental Free Trade Area. UBA also continues to pioneer digital innovation through LEO, Africa's first banking chatbot integrated with the PanAfrican Payment and Settlement System (PAPSS), enabling instant cross-border payments in local currencies. AWARDS 2025 saw exceptional recognition for the Group. UBA was named Nigeria's Strongest Brand by Brand Finance and ranked 13th Strongest Banking Brand Globally among the Top 500 Banks. The Banker (FT) awarded UBA Bank of the Year 2025 in nine African countries and the continental Bank of the Year Africa award. Additional recognition includes Best Corporate Governance Bank in Nigeria 2025, 3rd Most Gender Diverse NGX-30 Board (PWR Advisory), and four Asian Banker honours covering Leo, Merchant Plus, Best Mobile Banking App, and Best Digital Financial Inclusion Initiative in Africa. These achievements validate UBA's position as Africa's leading financial institution; demonstrated through sustained excellence, technological innovation, and an unwavering focus on customer satisfaction. OUTLOOK UBA's leadership positions today are the result of deliberate investment, disciplined execution, and the unwavering dedication of our people over many years. Looking ahead, UBA will continue expanding its presence across Africa and key global financial centres. Our priorities remain focused on strengthening our position in high-growth African markets, and expanding digital banking capabilities across our network. BOARD OF DIRECTORS During the year, Muyiwa Akinyemi, Abiola Bawuah, Alex Alozie, and Sola Yomi-Ajayi retired as Executive Directors from the Board following the successful completion of their tenures. On behalf of the Board and shareholders, I express our sincere appreciation for their dedication and contributions to the growth and governance of the Group. Subject to approval of the Central Bank of Nigeria, the Board
[TRUNCATED]

chairman_statement.text: CHAIRMAN Tony O. Elumelu is an African investor and philanthropist, and one of the world's most prominent voices on Africa's transformation agenda. He is the founder and Chairman of Heirs Holdings, his family-owned investment company, which invests in strategic sectors of the African economy, including financial services, hospitality, power, energy, technology, and healthcare, as part of its commitment to improving lives and transforming Africa. Tony is also the Chairman of the United Bank for Africa (UBA) Group, which operates in 20 countries across Africa, as well as the United Kingdom, France, the UAE, and the United States. He chairs Nigeria's largest quoted conglomerate, Transcorp Group, whose subsidiaries include Transcorp Power, a leading producer of electricity in West Africa, and Transcorp Hotels Plc, Nigeria's foremost hospitality brand. Together with Heirs Energies, one of the continent's largest natural resources producers, Transcorp Power is executing Africa's foremost integrated energy business, dedicated to ensuring African resources drive and power value creation on the African continent. In 2010, he created The Tony Elumelu Foundation (TEF), the leading philanthropy empowering a new generation of African entrepreneurs across all 54 African countries. Since inception, the Foundation's flagship programme has identified and funded over 20,000 young African entrepreneurs and created a digital ecosystem of over 1.5 million Africans, as part of a ten-year US$100m commitment to fund, mentor, and train young Africans. In 2020, TIME magazine named Tony as one of the 100 Most Influential People in the World and in 2022 TIME again recognised him with its inaugural TIME100 Impact list, honouring him alongside five other global leaders who have gone above and beyond to move their industries - and the world - forward. The Federal Government of Nigeria honoured Tony with the title, Commander of the Order of the Federal Republic, in 2022 for his contribution to Nigeria's economic and social development. He has also been recognised with Belgium's oldest and highest royal order. Tony sits on a number of global boards, including the Global Leadership Council of United Nations Generation Unlimited. Oliver Alawuba GROUP MANAGING DIRECTOR Oliver Alawuba is a seasoned banking professional with over 25 yearsʼ experience in Corporate and Institutional Banking, Consumer Banking, Public Sector, Retail and Commercial Banking, Project Management, Corporate Governance, and overall bank management, where he has demonstrated strong passion for excellence and result-oriented leadership capability. Prior to his current appointment as the Group Managing Director/CEO of UBA Plc, Oliver was at various times, CEO Designate for UBA Côte dʼIvoire, CEO UBA Ghana, Regional CEO, Anglophone Countries, Executive Director, East Bank in Nigeria,

ceo_review.text:
Managing Director/CEO UBA Africa, and later Group Deputy Managing Director/ CEO, UBA Africa covering Nigeria and other 19 Subsidiaries in UBA Africa (exNigeria). Presently as GMD/CEO of UBA Plc, he oversees the Global Operations of UBA across Nigeria, 19 UBA Africa Countries, UBA America, UBA United Kingdom, UBA France, and UBA UAE. He is a Fellow of the Nigeria Institute of Management (FNIM), and an Honorary Fellow of the Chartered Institute of Bankers of Nigeria (FCIB) He graduated from Imo State University, Okigwe (Now Abia State University, Uturu), with a BSc in Food Science & Technology in 1988. He also obtained an MSc in Food Technology from the University of Lagos in 1991 and MBA (Banking & Finance) from United Bank For Africa Plc // 2025 Annual Report and Accounts

Our Board and Executive Management | continued... Abdulqadir J. Bello, (FCA) NON-EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR Abdulqadir J. Bello is a Chartered Accountant, has over 30 yearsʼ corporate experience in the banking sector, during which period he held several senior Management positions in various Banks. He also previously served as the Group Chief Credit Officer of UBA and thereafter as the Executive Director in charge of Risk Management for UBA Group. Abdulqadir Bello is the Chairman of the Board Credit Committee and also serves on the Board Risk Management Committee. He obtained a BSc degree in Accounting from Bayero University, Kano in 1984 and ACA from the Institute of Chartered Accountants in 1989. Angela Adebayo NON-EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR Angela Adebayo obtained a BSc Hon (Social Science) from the University of Ibadan in 1979, an MBA from the University of Lagos in 1982, and MPhil (Cantab) in Land Economy from Cambridge University in 1994. Erelu Adebayo was previously the First Lady of Ekiti State and the Chairman of Afriland Properties Plc. She was also the first female Chairman of the Board of WEMABOD Estates. Erelu Adebayo serves on the Boards of Aliko Dangote Foundation, Meyer Paints Plc and Women at Risk International Foundation. She is also a Council Member on the Nigerian Stock Exchange and has worked extensively on real-estate development across Nigeria. Erelu Adebayo is the Founder of Erelu Adebayo Foundation and Erelu Adebayo Children's Home. Angela Aneke INDEPENDENT NON-EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR Angela Aneke is a board advisor, banker and a strategic thinker with over 30 yearsʼ experience in financial services, in the areas of financial control, strategy, transaction banking, corporate banking, retail banking, and governance. Ms. Aneke has held executive management and board positions in several international and regional institutions, including Ecobank Transnational Incorporated, Citigroup and the United Bank for Africa. Ms. Angela holds a Bachelor of Science degree from The American University, Washington DC, USA and became an International Associate of the American Institute of Certified Public Accountants. She also obtained a Master of Business Administration from Warwick Business School, University of Warwick, UK. She has attended several courses in banking and governance including Harvard Business Schoolʼs "Making Corporate Boards More Effective" and Whartonʼs University of Pennsylvaniaʼs Corporate Governance: "Maximize your effectiveness in the Board room." She is currently the Chairman of the Board Audit, Governance, Nomination & Remuneration Committee and also serves on the Board Credit Committee. United Bank For Africa Plc // 2025 Annual Report and Accounts

Corporate Profile Aisha Hassan Baba, OON INDEPENDENT NON-EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR Aisha Hassan Baba is the Founding and Managing Partner of EBO, HASSAN BABA & CO. Aisha was admitted to practice law in Nigeria in 1981 and in the ensuing 35 years thereafter, served in very senior and sensitive positions in both federal and state public service of the Federal Republic of Nigeria, notably as Deputy Director Public Prosecution, Director, Legal Services under the Federal Ministry of Justice, Federal Ministry of Education, Federal Ministry of Industry, Trade & Investment, Chief Executive Officer (CEO) of the Federal Legal Aid Council (now Commission), Executive Secretary, Nigerian Investment Promotion Commission, and as the Attorney General and Commissioner for Justice, Anambra State. Aisha is a trained Legal Draftsman, contract negotiator and has worked as Co-Chair of the Committee on High Profile Federal Bills, notably the production of the final draft copy of the National Competition and Consumer Protection Bill and the National Competition and Consumer Protection Policy in 2014/15. She has chaired several committees responsible for drafting a number of policies ranging from Nigerian Local Content in the non-oil Sector Policy 2014 to ; a model draft Automotive Bill for the Nigerian Automotive Council (as it then was) to finalise the draft Nigerian Insolvency Bill and so on. In recognition of her diligence, passion and contribution to the public service of the Federation, Aisha was conferred with the National Productivity Order of Merit Award in 2001 and the National Honours, Officer of the Order of the Niger, (OON) in 2005. Aisha is a member of the Nigerian Bar Association, Commonwealth Bar Association, Member, Chartered Institute of Arbitrators UK. Aisha currently serves on the Board Audit, Governance, Nomination & Remuneration Committee. Our Board and Executive Management | continued... Caroline Anyanwu NON-EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR Caroline Anyanwu has over 30 yearsʼ experience in the Banking Industry, covering Strategic Planning,Financial Control, Retail & Commercial Banking, Banking Operations and Risk Management. She is a First Class graduate of Statistics from University of Ilorin in 1984, a Fellow of the Institute of Chartered Accountants (ICAN), and a Prize Winner in the ICAN Professional Qualifying Examination (overall 2nd). She obtained top-rated core basic and intermediate management competencies as a Trainee Accountant in Price Waterhouse (Chartered Accountants) - now PricewaterhouseCoopers (PwC). Caroline who previously occupied the position of Head, Credit Risk Management at United Bank for Africa Plc, has worked at senior level positions with several international financial institutions. An Honorary Senior Member of the Chartered Institute of Bankers of Nigeria, an Associate Member of the Chartered Institute of Taxation of Nigeria, and a member of Caroline Anyanwu the Risk Management Association of Nigeria. United Bank For Africa Plc // 2025 Annual Report and Accounts

Chukwuma Nweke DEPUTY MANAGING DIRECTOR, OPERATIONS, TECHNOLOGY & DIGITAL BANKING Chukwuma Nweke has over 30 years of experience across Banking Operations, Finance, Technology, Audit, Strategy, Retail Banking, and Payments, Mr. Nweke previously served as Executive Director and Group Chief Operating Officer of UBA Plc. Mr. Nweke holds a Bachelorʼs degree in Accountancy and an MBA from the University of Nigeria, Nsukka. He is a Fellow of the Institute of Chartered Accountants of Nigeria (FCA) and an honorary member of the Chartered Institute of Bankers of Nigeria. Henrietta Ugboh INDEPENDENT NON-EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR Henrietta Ugboh is a board advisor, financial literacy and risk management trainer, mentor and banker with 30 yearsʼ experience in financial services, in the areas of corporate and commercial banking, credit and risk management, private banking and governance. Mrs. Ugboh has held senior management and board positions in several institutions, including Citibank Nigeria Limited, Investment One Financial Services, Harvard Business School Association of Nigeria (HBSAN) and Ziphii Technologies. Hetty holds a Bachelor of Science degree in Economics and Statistics from the University of Benin, Benin City and a Masters of Business Administration from Enugu State University of Technology (ESUT) Business School. She is an alumna of the prestigious Harvard Business School, Boston and attended several courses in banking, leadership and governance including Citibank Country Managerʼs Forum (New York), Citigroup Risk and Compliance trainings, John Maxwell leadership Certification Program (Florida), Institute of Directors - Company Direction Course (Lagos) and African Angel Academy training. She is an Honorary Senior Member of the Chartered Institute of Bankers of Nigeria, Member, Institute of Directors and Fellow of the Institute of Credit Administration (FICA). Emmanuel Nnorom NON-EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR Emmanuel Nnorom is a qualified Chartered Accountant and has over 4 decades of professional experience in the corporate and financial sectors, working with publicly listed companies. He is an alumnus of Oxford Universityʼs Templeton College (1996), and a prize winner at the Foundation, Professional Examinations 1 and Profession Examinations 2 qualifying examinations in 1980,1981 and 1982 and Fellow of the Institute of Chartered Accountants of Nigeria. He trained at Peak Marwick Casselton Elliot (later Peat Marwick, Ani, Ogunde & Co) from 1978 to 1982 and also worked with Nigerian Breweries Plc as well as several financial institutions before retiring from Standard Trust Bank/UBA Plc. Emmanuel N. Nnorom is the CEO of Heirs Holdings Ltd. Prior to joining the Heirs Holdings Group, he served as the CEO of Transnational Corporation of Nigeria Plc, overseeing the Transcorp businesses, including Transcorp Power, Transcorp Hilton Hotels Abuja, Transcorp Hotel, Calabar and Transcorp Energy. Prior to Transcorp, Emmanuel had held senior positions at Heirs Holdings and had served as an Executive Director at UBA Plc and Managing Director of UBA Africa, overseeing the Groupʼs African subsidiaries and executing corporate strategy in 18 African countries.Other senior roles within UBA Plc included Group COO UBA Plc, followed by his appointment as UBAʼs Group CFO, with responsibility for Finance and Risk. Our Board and Executive Management | continued... United Bank For Africa Plc // 2025 Annual Report and Accounts

Corporate Profile Ugochukwu Nwaghodoh EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR, FINANCE AND RISK MANAGEMENT Ugochukwu Nwaghodoh has over 30 years of multifunctional experience spanning banking, advisory and assurance services. Prior to his current role, he was at different times, Group Financial Controller, Group Chief Compliance Officer, and Head - Performance Management at the UBA Group. Before joining UBA in 2004, he had a decade of experience with renowned firms of Deloitte and PricewaterhouseCoopers in Nigeria and Kenya. He holds a BSc in Human Nutrition from the University of Ibadan, MSc in Finance and Management from Cranfield University, and an MSc in Risk Management from New York University. He is an alumnus of the Oxford University's Said Business School, and Institute of Management Development (IMD) Switzerland. Ugo is a fellow of the Institute of Chartered Accountants of Nigeria, a fellow of the Chartered Institute of Taxation of Nigeria (CITN), an Associate member of the Chartered Institute of Stockbrokers of Nigeria, a member of Chartered Institute for Securities and Investments, United Kingdom and an Honorary Senior Member of the Chartered Institute of Bankers of Nigeria (CIBN). He is also a member of the Institute of Directors of Nigeria and holds the Financial Times Non-Executive Director Diploma. Bili A. Odum GROUP COMPANY SECRETARY/ LEGAL COUNSEL Bili A. Odum holds an LLB (Hons) degree from Edo State University, Ekpoma, Nigeria and was enrolled as a Solicitor and Advocate of the Supreme Court of Nigeria in 1990. He is a member of the Chartered Institute of Arbitrators (United Kingdom), the Nigerian Bar Association and the International Bar Association. He is an alumnus of the Lagos Business School (Chief Executive Programme 18) and the New York Institute of Finance. He has held high-level strategic positions in top financial service institutions in Nigeria, with responsibilities that encompass Asset Management, Structured Finance, Legal Advisory, Corporate Governance, Human Resources Management, Administration, Knowledge Management and Business Communication. Our Board and Executive Management | continued... United Bank For Africa Plc // 2025 Annual Report and Accounts

outlook.text:
OUTLOOK UBA's leadership positions today are the result of deliberate investment, disciplined execution, and the unwavering dedication of our people over many years. Looking ahead, UBA will continue expanding its presence across Africa and key global financial centres. Our priorities remain focused on strengthening our position in high-growth African markets, and expanding digital banking capabilities across our network. BOARD OF DIRECTORS During the year, Muyiwa Akinyemi, Abiola Bawuah, Alex Alozie, and Sola Yomi-Ajayi retired as Executive Directors from the Board following the successful completion of their tenures. On behalf of the Board and shareholders, I express our sincere appreciation for their dedication and contributions to the growth and governance of the Group. Subject to approval of the Central Bank of Nigeria, the Board welcomed Emmanuel Lamptey, Tosin Adewuyi, and Chidi Okpala as Executive Directors. Their experience and leadership will further strengthen the Group as we continue to execute our long-term strategy. ACKNOWLEDGEMENTS On behalf of the Board, I extend my sincere appreciation to management and staff for their dedication and commitment throughout the year. We also thank our customers for their continued trust, our regulators for their guidance, and our shareholders for their confidence in UBA's long-term vision. Tony O. Elumelu, CFR Chairman, Board of Directors

United Bank For Africa Plc // 2025 Annual Report and Accounts

Oliver Alawuba Group Managing Director/CEO

Strategy & Business Review CEO's Statement A good number of our subsidiaries are also among the top three in key performance metrics in their respective markets, with several attaining Systemically Important Bank status. Distinguished Shareholders, I am pleased to present United Bank for Africa Plc's Annual Report and Audited Financial Statements for the year ended December 31, 2025. We deeply appreciate the confidence, partnership and continued belief of our shareholders, customers, regulators, partners, analysts and staff across the 24 countries where UBA operates in the long-term strength of Africa's Global Bank. The year was defined by macroeconomic adjustment, elevated funding costs, exchange-rate pressure, inflationary headwinds and uncertainty across several markets. Yet UBA preserved the strengths that have always defined this institution: resilience, scale, diversification, disciplined execution and customer trust. Across our footprint, we stayed anchored on our 3Es of Enterprise, Excellence and Execution, while strengthening governance, risk discipline and institutional capacity for the future A Year of Resilience in the midst of pressure UBA delivered strong gross earnings of N3.1 trillion in 2025, reaffirming the depth of our franchise, the resilience of our business model and the strength of our core banking operations. Net interest income remained strong at N1.62 trillion, while profit before tax and profit after tax closed at N423.4 billion and N404.7 billion respectively, despite a difficult operating cycle. Our 2025 result was impacted by material loan-loss provisions of N331 billion and fair value changes on derivatives of N227 billion. Importantly, these are one-off, non-recurring charges. We have further fortified our recovery architecture, with a strengthened recovery team aggressively pursuing the affected facilities. Every recovery achieved will flow directly through the Profit and Loss Account in FY2026 and beyond, providing additional upside to earnings. The Group continued to grow strategically. Customer deposits increased by 11.8% to N27.2 trillion, while total assets rose by 9.4% to N33.2 trillion. Total equity stood at N4.25 trillion, with equity attributable to shareholders at N4.12 trillion. These are clear indicators of customer confidence, balance sheet strength and the enduring relevance of the UBA franchise. Strength of Our Pan-African Franchise Our pan-African operations remained a major growth engine and a powerful differentiator. Operating in 20 African countries, alongside our presence in the United States, United Kingdom, France and the United Arab Emirates, UBA remains uniquely positioned to connect Africa to global capital, trade, payments and investment flows. The Group's African subsidiaries continued to contribute over 50% of Group assets, revenue and profit, validating the strength of our diversified model across geographies. In 2025, West Africa recorded 53% profit growth, while East and Southern Africa delivered 61% profit growth, reinforcing the resilience, scalability and long-term value of our African platform. A good number of our subsidiaries are also among the top three in key performance metrics in their respective markets, with several attaining Systemically Important Bank status. These outcomes reaffirm the strategic value of our geographic diversification and the capacity of our African network to drive sustainable shareholder value. Stronger Capital, Stronger Optionality A major milestone of the year was the successful completion of our Rights Issue. The Bank's share capital and premium now amount to N504 billion, while shareholders' funds stood at N4.25 trillion, supported by a strong Capital Adequacy Ratio of 23.2%. This gives UBA the capacity, flexibility and confidence to pursue significant growth opportunities across priority markets and sectors. At the same time, our reserves profile improved materially, further fortifying the Bank. As macroeconomic fundamentals strengthen, UBA is well positioned to grow its risk asset base prudently in choice sectors, deepen trade finance, expand transaction banking and support high-quality opportunities across our markets, thereby enhancing earnings and profitability in FY2026 and beyond. The Franchise Remains Strong Beyond the numbers, the franchise remains exceptionally United Bank For Africa Plc // 2025 Annual Report and Accounts

CEO's Statement continued... strong. We serve over 45 million customers across 24 countries, supported by a trusted brand, deep customer relationships and a diversified operating platform. Our share price closed 2025 at N41.65, up 22.5% year-on-year, with 6.78 billion shares traded during the year, reflecting continuing investor interest, liquidity and confidence in the long-term value of the institution. We also continued to strengthen customer experience and trust. Our complaint-to-transaction ratio improved to 0.03% from 0.04% in the prior year, showing that even while navigating a difficult environment, we kept listening, responding and improving. Consistent with our Customer First philosophy, the customer will always remain central to our operations. Brand Leadership and Market Recognition In 2025, UBA's brand performance was exceptional. We were named Nigeria's Strongest Brand, rising from 9th to 1st place within one year, and ranked the 13th strongest banking brand globally by Brand Finance. For the third time in five years, UBA also emerged African Bank of the Year 2025 by The Banker, winning Bank of the Year awards in nine (9) African countries: Benin, Chad, Mali, Senegal, Congo Brazzaville, Liberia, Sierra Leone, Mozambique and Zambia. These recognitions affirm the strength of our brand, the loyalty of our customers, the quality of our execution and the relevance of UBA's pan-African platform. In line with our C1st Philosophy and UBA Persona of Simplicity, Responsiveness and Goal-oriented (SRG), our goal remains clear: make banking easier to use, easier to trust and easier to recommend across channels and borders Building a Better 2026 In 2025, the reconstitution of Board Committees from four to five enhanced governance depth and oversight. Deloitte's evaluation confirmed compliance with extant corporate

Tony sits on a number of global boards, including the Global Leadership Council of United Nations Generation Unlimited. Oliver Alawuba GROUP MANAGING DIRECTOR Oliver Alawuba is a seasoned banking professional with over 25 yearsʼ experience in Corporate and Institutional Banking, Consumer Banking, Public Sector, Retail and Commercial Banking, Project Management, Corporate Governance, and overall bank management, where he has demonstrated strong passion for excellence and result-oriented leadership capability. Prior to his current appointment as the Group Managing Director/CEO of UBA Plc, Oliver was at various times, CEO Designate for UBA Côte dʼIvoire, CEO UBA Ghana, Regional CEO, Anglophone Countries, Executive Director, East Bank in Nigeria,

Managing Director/CEO UBA Africa, and later Group Deputy Managing Director/ CEO, UBA Africa covering Nigeria and other 19 Subsidiaries in UBA Africa (exNigeria). Presently as GMD/CEO of UBA Plc, he oversees the Global Operations of UBA across Nigeria, 19 UBA Africa Countries, UBA America, UBA United Kingdom, UBA France, and UBA UAE. He is a Fellow of the Nigeria Institute of Management (FNIM), and an Honorary Fellow of the Chartered Institute of Bankers of Nigeria (FCIB) He graduated from Imo State University, Okigwe (Now Abia State University, Uturu), with a BSc in Food Science & Technology in 1988. He also obtained an MSc in Food Technology from the University of Lagos in 1991 and MBA (Banking & Finance) from United Bank For Africa Plc // 2025 Annual Report and Accounts

Our Board and Executive Management | continued... Abdulqadir J. Bello, (FCA) NON-EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR Abdulqadir J. Bello is a Chartered Accountant, has over 30 yearsʼ corporate experience in the banking sector, during which period he held several senior Management positions in various Banks. He also previously served as the Group Chief Credit Officer of UBA and thereafter as the Executive Director in charge of Risk Management for UBA Group. Abdulqadir Bello is the Chairman of the Board Credit Committee and also serves on the Board Risk Management Committee. He obtained a BSc degree in Accounting from Bayero University, Kano in 1984 and ACA from the Institute of Chartered Accountants in 1989. Angela Adebayo NON-EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR Angela Adebayo obtained a BSc Hon (Social Science) from the University of Ibadan in 1979, an MBA from the University of Lagos in 1982, and MPhil (Cantab) in Land Economy from Cambridge University in 1994. Erelu Adebayo was previously the First Lady of Ekiti State and the Chairman of Afriland Properties Plc.

She was also the first female Chairman of the Board of WEMABOD Estates. Erelu Adebayo serves on the Boards of Aliko Dangote Foundation, Meyer Paints Plc and Women at Risk International Foundation. She is also a Council Member on the Nigerian Stock Exchange and has worked extensively on real-estate development across Nigeria. Erelu Adebayo is the Founder of Erelu Adebayo Foundation and Erelu Adebayo Children's Home. Angela Aneke INDEPENDENT NON-EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR Angela Aneke is a board advisor, banker and a strategic thinker with over 30 yearsʼ experience in financial services, in the areas of financial control, strategy, transaction banking, corporate banking, retail banking, and governance. Ms. Aneke has held executive management and board positions in several international and regional institutions, including Ecobank Transnational Incorporated, Citigroup and the United Bank for Africa. Ms. Angela holds a Bachelor of Science degree from The American University, Washington DC, USA and became an International Associate of the American Institute of Certified Public Accountants.

She also obtained a Master of Business Administration from Warwick Business School, University of Warwick, UK. She has attended several courses in banking and governance including Harvard Business Schoolʼs "Making Corporate Boards More Effective" and Whartonʼs University of Pennsylvaniaʼs Corporate Governance: "Maximize your effectiveness in the Board room." She is currently the Chairman of the Board Audit, Governance, Nomination & Remuneration Committee and also serves on the Board Credit Committee. United Bank For Africa Plc // 2025 Annual Report and Accounts

Corporate Profile Aisha Hassan Baba, OON INDEPENDENT NON-EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR Aisha Hassan Baba is the Founding and Managing Partner of EBO, HASSAN BABA & CO. Aisha was admitted to practice law in Nigeria in 1981 and in the ensuing 35 years thereafter, served in very senior and sensitive positions in both federal and state public service of the Federal Republic of Nigeria, notably as Deputy Director Public Prosecution, Director, Legal Services under the Federal Ministry of Justice, Federal Ministry of Education, Federal Ministry of Industry, Trade & Investment, Chief Executive Officer (CEO) of the Federal Legal Aid Council (now Commission), Executive Secretary, Nigerian Investment Promotion Commission, and as the Attorney General and Commissioner for Justice, Anambra State. Aisha is a trained Legal Draftsman, contract negotiator and has worked as Co-Chair of the Committee on High Profile Federal Bills, notably the production of the final draft copy of the National Competition and Consumer Protection Bill and the National Competition and Consumer Protection Policy in 2014/15.

She has chaired several committees responsible for drafting a number of policies ranging from Nigerian Local Content in the non-oil Sector Policy 2014 to ; a model draft Automotive Bill for the Nigerian Automotive Council (as it then was) to finalise the draft Nigerian Insolvency Bill and so on. In recognition of her diligence, passion and contribution to the public service of the Federation, Aisha was conferred with the National Productivity Order of Merit Award in 2001 and the National Honours, Officer of the Order of the Niger, (OON) in 2005. Aisha is a member of the Nigerian Bar Association, Commonwealth Bar Association, Member, Chartered Institute of Arbitrators UK. Aisha currently serves on the Board Audit, Governance, Nomination & Remuneration Committee. Our Board and Executive Management | continued... Caroline Anyanwu NON-EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR Caroline Anyanwu has over 30 yearsʼ experience in the Banking Industry, covering Strategic Planning,Financial Control, Retail & Commercial Banking, Banking Operations and Risk Management.

She is a First Class graduate of Statistics from University of Ilorin in 1984, a Fellow of the Institute of Chartered Accountants (ICAN), and a Prize Winner in the ICAN Professional Qualifying Examination (overall 2nd). She obtained top-rated core basic and intermediate management competencies as a Trainee Accountant in Price Waterhouse (Chartered Accountants) - now PricewaterhouseCoopers (PwC). Caroline who previously occupied the position of Head, Credit Risk Management at United Bank for Africa Plc, has worked at senior level positions with several international financial institutions. An Honorary Senior Member of the Chartered Institute of Bankers of Nigeria, an Associate Member of the Chartered Institute of Taxation of Nigeria, and a member of Caroline Anyanwu the Risk Management Association of Nigeria. United Bank For Africa Plc // 2025 Annual Report and Accounts

Chukwuma Nweke DEPUTY MANAGING DIRECTOR, OPERATIONS, TECHNOLOGY & DIGITAL BANKING Chukwuma Nweke has over 30 years of experience across Banking Operations, Finance, Technology, Audit, Strategy, Retail Banking, and Payments, Mr. Nweke previously served as Executive Director and Group Chief Operating Officer of UBA Plc. Mr. Nweke holds a Bachelorʼs degree in Accountancy and an MBA from the University of Nigeria, Nsukka. He is a Fellow of the Institute of Chartered Accountants of Nigeria (FCA) and an honorary member of the Chartered Institute of Bankers of Nigeria. Henrietta Ugboh INDEPENDENT NON-EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR Henrietta Ugboh is a board advisor, financial literacy and risk management trainer, mentor and banker with 30 yearsʼ experience in financial services, in the areas of corporate and commercial banking, credit and risk management, private banking and governance. Mrs. Ugboh has held senior management and board positions in several institutions, including Citibank Nigeria Limited, Investment One Financial Services, Harvard Business School Association of Nigeria (HBSAN) and Ziphii Technologies.

Hetty holds a Bachelor of Science degree in Economics and Statistics from the University of Benin, Benin City and a Masters of Business Administration from Enugu State University of Technology (ESUT) Business School. She is an alumna of the prestigious Harvard Business School, Boston and attended several courses in banking, leadership and governance including Citibank Country Managerʼs Forum (New York), Citigroup Risk and Compliance trainings, John Maxwell leadership Certification Program (Florida), Institute of Directors - Company Direction Course (Lagos) and African Angel Academy training. She is an Honorary Senior Member of the Chartered Institute of Bankers of Nigeria, Member, Institute of Directors and Fellow of the Institute of Credit Administration (FICA). Emmanuel Nnorom NON-EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR Emmanuel Nnorom is a qualified Chartered Accountant and has over 4 decades of professional experience in the corporate and financial sectors, working with publicly listed companies. He is an alumnus of Oxford Universityʼs Templeton College (1996), and a prize winner at the Foundation, Professional Examinations 1 and Profession Examinations 2 qualifying examinations in 1980,1981 and 1982 and Fellow of the Institute of Chartered Accountants of Nigeria.

He trained at Peak Marwick Casselton Elliot (later Peat Marwick, Ani, Ogunde & Co) from 1978 to 1982 and also worked with Nigerian Breweries Plc as well as several financial institutions before retiring from Standard Trust Bank/UBA Plc. Emmanuel N. Nnorom is the CEO of Heirs Holdings Ltd. Prior to joining the Heirs Holdings Group, he served as the CEO of Transnational Corporation of Nigeria Plc, overseeing the Transcorp businesses, including Transcorp Power, Transcorp Hilton Hotels Abuja, Transcorp Hotel, Calabar and Transcorp Energy. Prior to Transcorp, Emmanuel had held senior positions at Heirs Holdings and had served as an Executive Director at UBA Plc and Managing Director of UBA Africa, overseeing the Groupʼs African subsidiaries and executing corporate strategy in 18 African countries.Other senior roles within UBA Plc included Group COO UBA Plc, followed by his appointment as UBAʼs Group CFO, with responsibility for Finance and Risk. Corporate Profile Ugochukwu Nwaghodoh EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR, FINANCE AND RISK MANAGEMENT Ugochukwu Nwaghodoh has over 30 years of multifunctional experience spanning banking, advisory and assurance services. Prior to his current role, he was at different times, Group Financial Controller, Group Chief Compliance Officer, and Head - Performance Management at the UBA Group. Before joining UBA in 2004, he had a decade of experience with renowned firms of Deloitte and PricewaterhouseCoopers in Nigeria and Kenya. He holds a BSc in Human Nutrition from the University of Ibadan, MSc in Finance and Management from Cranfield University, and an MSc in Risk Management from New York University. He is an alumnus of the Oxford University's Said Business School, and Institute of Management Development (IMD) Switzerland. Ugo is a fellow of the Institute of Chartered Accountants of Nigeria, a fellow of the Chartered Institute of Taxation of Nigeria (CITN), an Associate member of the Chartered Institute of Stockbrokers of Nigeria, a member of Chartered Institute for Securities and Investments, United Kingdom and an Honorary Senior Member of the Chartered Institute of Bankers of Nigeria (CIBN). He is also a member of the Institute of Directors of Nigeria and holds the Financial Times Non-Executive Director Diploma. Bili A. Odum GROUP COMPANY SECRETARY/ LEGAL COUNSEL Bili A. Odum holds an LLB (Hons) degree from Edo State University, Ekpoma, Nigeria and was enrolled as a Solicitor and Advocate of the Supreme Court of Nigeria in 1990. He is a member of the Chartered Institute of Arbitrators (United Kingdom), the Nigerian Bar Association and the International Bar Association. He is an alumnus of the Lagos Business School (Chief Executive Programme 18) and the New York Institute of Finance. He has held high-level strategic positions in top financial service institutions in Nigeria, with responsibilities that encompass Asset Management, Structured Finance, Legal Advisory, Corporate Governance, Human Resources Management, Administration, Knowledge Management and Business Communication.
